root_cause.text: The system was vulnerable to insecure deserialization because it did not broadly blacklist classes by default during object deserialization, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces `tomee.serialization.class.blacklist = *` as the default in `Installer.java` and modifies the `contains` method in `BlacklistClassResolver` and `EjbObjectInputStream` to properly evaluate the `*` wildcard to block all classes unless explicitly whitelisted.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The system was vulnerable to insecure deserialization because it did not broadly blacklist classes by default during object deserialization, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces `tomee.serialization.class.blacklist = *` as the default in `Installer.java` and modifies the `contains` method in `BlacklistClassResolver` and `EjbObjectInputStream` to properly evaluate the `*` wildcard to block all classes unless explicitly whitelisted.

intent_record.intent_summary: Configure serialization properties to allow all classes when unsafe EJBD mode is enabled.
intent_record.what: Added a conditional block that removes the serialization blacklist and sets the whitelist to a wildcard if unsafeEjbd is enabled.
intent_record.how: Modifies the serialization properties at runtime to allow all classes to be serialized when the unsafe EJBD mode is explicitly requested by the configuration.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unsafeEjbd configuration property to TomEEConfiguration.
intent_record.what: Added a new boolean field 'unsafeEjbd' initialized to true in the TomEEConfiguration class.
intent_record.how: Introduces a new configuration flag to control EJBD behavior, making it accessible to other parts of the system.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add getter and setter for the unsafeEjbd configuration property.
intent_record.what: Added getter and setter methods for the unsafeEjbd boolean property.
intent_record.how: Exposes the unsafeEjbd property so it can be read and modified by other components or configuration frameworks.

intent_record.intent_summary: Adjusts serialization configuration to explicitly allow all classes when unsafe EJBD mode is active.
intent_record.what: Replaces a simple properties return with conditional logic that alters blacklist and whitelist properties.
intent_record.how: Checks if unsafe EJBD is enabled and the blacklist is a wildcard, then removes the blacklist and explicitly sets the whitelist to a wildcard.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add wildcard support to the class resolver.
intent_record.what: Added a check to see if the list element is a wildcard ('*').
intent_record.how: Allows the method to return true immediately if a wildcard is present, effectively matching any name.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test case to verify wildcard blacklisting behavior.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method named wildcard().
intent_record.how: Verifies that the BlacklistClassResolver correctly blacklists classes when configured with a wildcard.

intent_record.intent_summary: Support wildcard character in the class name filtering list.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to check if the list element is a wildcard ('*') before checking if the name starts with it.
intent_record.how: This allows the application to process wildcard entries in the list, enabling it to match all incoming names when a '*' is present.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add default serialization blacklist property to secure deserialization.
intent_record.what: Added default system properties for serialization blacklisting.
intent_record.how: Secures the default configuration by denying all deserialization packages.

grouping_result.intent: Add getter and setter for the unsafeEjbd configuration property.
Support wildcard character in the class name filtering list.
Add test case to verify wildcard blacklisting behavior.
Add default serialization blacklist property to secure deserialization.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add and configure the unsafeEjbd property in TomEEConfiguration.
Implement and configure wildcard support for class name filtering in serialization.

relevance_review.reason: The group introduces the 'unsafeEjbd' configuration, which determines whether all classes are allowed during serialization. This directly relates to addressing the insecure deserialization vulnerability caused by the lack of default class blacklisting.

The group introduces wildcard support for class name filtering during serialization, which directly addresses the root cause of insecure deserialization by allowing broad class blacklisting or whitelisting.
The group adds a test case specifically for wildcard blacklisting, which directly verifies the fix for the insecure deserialization vulnerability caused by the lack of broad class blacklisting.
The group directly addresses the root cause by adding a default serialization blacklist property, which mitigates the insecure deserialization vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This statement performs a basic prefix match without wildcard support, preventing broad blacklisting of classes.
This statement performs a basic prefix match in the client object input stream without wildcard support, preventing broad blacklisting.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement restricts blacklisting/whitelisting to basic prefix matching without wildcard support, preventing the ability to broadly blacklist classes by default, which is necessary to prevent insecure deserialization.

Similarly, in the client object input stream, this statement restricts matching to simple prefixes, limiting the effectiveness of the class blacklist and allowing potentially dangerous classes to be deserialized.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 3966347fd36f613981a0ed8a7bce1a8da7c31dd5
Author: Romain Manni-Bucau <rmannibu@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Nov 27 12:57:36 2015 +0100

    TOMEE-1669 blacklist org.codehaus.groovy.runtime.,org.apache.commons.collections.functors.,org.apache.xalan in our custom ObjectInputStream

diff --git a/container/openejb-core/src/main/java/org/apache/openejb/core/rmi/BlacklistClassResolver.java b/container/openejb-core/src/main/java/org/apache/openejb/core/rmi/BlacklistClassResolver.java
new file mode 1006
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces a new file `BlacklistClassResolver.java` which implements the fix by creating a class resolver that checks class names against a blacklist (including org.codehaus.groovy.runtime., org.apache.commons.collections.functors., and org.apache.xalan) and throws a SecurityException if a blacklisted class is encountered. Thus, this code contains the fix.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Set the classloader of the bean on the thread for deserialization


git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/incubat
bic_trace_step.reason: The resolveClass method simply calls Class.forName without any blacklist checks, which allows arbitrary classes to be deserialized, leading to the insecure deserialization vulnerability. The fix, which would involve restricting which classes can be resolved, is not present.
